summary: Linked Jigglypuff/Purin `ftPr_SpecialHi.c` as a 1.22 KB matching C slice. The PR split the former large `ftPr_SpecialHi` asm/source region at `0x8013CE8C`, leaving Up-B/SpecialHi in a now-matching C object and moving the remaining Down-B/Side-B/Neutral-B region into `ftPr_SpecialLw.c` plus renamed residual asm. The report shows all listed SpecialHi functions at 100.00% with score 0.
what_changed: `configure.py` changed Purin `ftPr_SpecialHi.c` from `NonMatching` to `Matching` and added `ftPr_SpecialLw.c` as the new `NonMatching` object.

`obj_files.mk` stopped building `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialHi.s.o`, started building `src/.../ftPr_SpecialHi.c.o`, and added `asm/.../ftPr_SpecialLw.s.o` for the remaining unmatched region.

`config/GALE01/splits.txt` narrowed `ftPr_SpecialHi.c` text from `0x8013C9C8-0x801422E8` to `0x8013C9C8-0x8013CE8C`, assigned its sdata2 constants to `0x804D9C10-0x804D9C1C`, and created a new `ftPr_SpecialLw.c` split from `0x8013CE8C-0x801422E8` with later sdata/sdata2 ranges.

The old asm file was effectively renamed from `ftPr_SpecialHi.s` to `ftPr_SpecialLw.s`; the SpecialHi assembly was removed from it, leaving the residual unmatched Purin special-move region beginning at `ftPr_SpecialLw_Enter`.
`ftPr_SpecialHi.c` was reduced to the SpecialHi functions only, with later SpecialLw/SpecialS/SpecialN code removed into the new `ftPr_SpecialLw.c` source file.
`ftPr_SpecialHi.c` replaced extern float constants `ftPr_Init_804D9C10`, `ftPr_Init_804D9C14`, and `ftPr_Init_804D9C18` with literal `-1`, `0`, and `1` in the matching SpecialHi motion-state calls.
`ftPr_SpecialHi.h` was trimmed to only SpecialHi declarations and dropped many downstream SpecialLw/SpecialS/SpecialN prototypes.
A new `ftPr_SpecialLw.h` was added with declarations for the remaining region beginning at `ftPr_SpecialLw_Enter` and continuing through later Purin special functions.
`ftPr_Init.c` and `ftdata.c` added includes for `ftPr_SpecialLw.h` so declarations remained available after the header split.
smart_moves: Used a split-boundary adjustment rather than trying to link the entire old Purin special-move file at once; isolating only `0x8013C9C8-0x8013CE8C` made the small SpecialHi slice linkable.
Preserved the unmatched tail by renaming the residual asm/object to `ftPr_SpecialLw`, avoiding disruption to the still-nonmatching Down-B/Side-B/Neutral-B region.
Kept build metadata, split metadata, source/header organization, and include sites synchronized in the same PR, which is important when carving a matching file out of a larger asm-backed blob.
Simplified SpecialHi constants to direct literals for `-1`, `0`, and `1`, which matched while allowing the later SpecialLw region to keep using its own `ftPr_Init_804D9C20/24/28` extern constants.
Trimmed `ftPr_SpecialHi.h` so the linked file no longer advertised unrelated later Purin special-move declarations.
decomp_lessons: When a source file covers multiple contiguous move families, a small matching PR can link only the leading matched range by changing `splits.txt` and moving the unmatched remainder to a new source/asm identity.
For fighter special moves, always update all three build layers together: `splits.txt`, `configure.py` matching status, and `obj_files.mk` object path.
Header splits must follow code splits; otherwise init tables and `ftdata.c` may still depend on prototypes that were removed from the old header.
Literal float constants can be preferable to named sdata2 externs when the split assigns the constants to the newly matched file and the generated code still matches.
The matching report is strong evidence here: every listed `ftPr_SpecialHi` function, including `Enter`, `Anim`, `IASA`, `Phys`, `Coll`, and helper callbacks, reports score 0 / 100.00%.
naming_conventions: Purin/Jigglypuff files use the `ftPr_` prefix, e.g. `ftPr_SpecialHi.c`, `ftPr_SpecialLw.c`, and `ftPr_Init.c`.
Ground and air variants are named with `SpecialHi` and `SpecialAirHi`, e.g. `ftPr_SpecialHi_Enter` and `ftPr_SpecialAirHi_Enter`.
Unknown or not-yet-semantic helper names retain address suffixes, e.g. `ftPr_SpecialHi_8013CD34`, `ftPr_SpecialHi_8013CDD8`, and `ftPr_SpecialHi_8013CE7C`.
Motion state names encode direction with `L` and `R`, such as `ftPr_MS_SpecialHiL`, `ftPr_MS_SpecialHiR`, `ftPr_MS_SpecialAirHiL`, and `ftPr_MS_SpecialAirHiR`.
Header guards use address-based `GALE01_...` identifiers, e.g. `GALE01_13C9C8` for `ftPr_SpecialHi.h` and `GALE01_13CE8C` for `ftPr_SpecialLw.h`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the matched SpecialHi assembly from the residual asm file and started the remaining asm at `0x8013CE8C` with `.file "ftPr_SpecialLw.c"`.
Changed the SpecialHi split to own `.text 0x8013C9C8-0x8013CE8C` and `.sdata2 0x804D9C10-0x804D9C1C`; shifted the leftover SpecialLw split to `.sdata2 0x804D9C20-0x804D9C98`.
Matched short IASA functions as empty/no-op functions and Phys functions as direct wrappers around common physics helpers (`ft_80084F3C`, `ft_80084EEC`).
Used common animation-ending idioms: `ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining` followed by ground/air transition helpers such as `ft_8008A2BC` and `ftCo_800CC730`.
Ground/air collision transitions preserved current animation frame and motion flags with `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, including `0x0C4C508A` for SpecialHi state transfers.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that link `ftPr_SpecialLw.c` or split the remaining Purin special-move region.
Search for additional cleanup of `ftPr_SpecialLw.h`, especially moving SpecialS and SpecialN prototypes into more specific headers.
Compare other fighter PRs that carve a matching leading text range out of a larger nonmatching asm blob.
Check whether later naming work replaces `ftPr_SpecialHi_8013CD34`, `ftPr_SpecialHi_8013CDD8`, or `ftPr_SpecialHi_8013CE7C` with semantic names.
